content.summary: Maps incoming OSC (Open Sound Control) messages to TouchDesigner operator parameters for remote control from tablets, phones, or other software. An OSC In CHOP receives messages, a Select CHOP isolates specific addresses, and CHOP Exports or Python callbacks bind values to target parameters. This enables wireless control surfaces, inter-application communication, and audience interaction.

operatorChain.role: monitor raw OSC messages for debugging

codeSnippets.label: Python callback for OSC message routing
codeSnippets.description: Routes incoming OSC messages to different operator parameters based on the OSC address pattern.

codeSnippets.label: Configure OSC In CHOP for TouchOSC
codeSnippets.code:
# Set up OSC In CHOP to receive from TouchOSC
osc_in = op('oscin1')
osc_in.par.port = 9000
osc_in.par.active = True
codeSnippets.description: Configures the OSC In CHOP to listen on port 9000, the default TouchOSC send port.

tips: Use an OSC In DAT alongside the OSC In CHOP to monitor raw messages for debugging address patterns.
Apply a Filter CHOP after the OSC In to smooth out jittery controller data.
Use a Math CHOP to remap incoming 0-1 ranges to the parameter ranges you need.
Set the OSC In CHOP network port to match your sending application (TouchOSC default is 9000).
